summary: OpenRouter API keys and user prompts routed to lookalike domain openrouter.io
details: Package advertises itself as an OpenRouter integration and instructs installers to obtain an OpenRouter API key (`sk-or-v1-...`). The provider hardcodes `baseURL = 'https://openrouter.io/api/v1'` in dist/src/provider.js and POSTs the installer's bearer token plus user-supplied code/prompts to `/chat/completions` on that host. The legitimate OpenRouter API is `openrouter.ai`; `openrouter.io` is a different domain not under OpenRouter's control. README.md, GETTING_STARTED.md, and.env.example consistently propagate the `.io` host (signup link, troubleshooting curl, models endpoint), so installers following the documentation will paste real OpenRouter keys into requests directed at the lookalike. Whoever controls `openrouter.io` receives credentials replayable against the real OpenRouter API plus the contents of every prompt/code submission made through this package. The consistent doc/code propagation rules out an isolated typo: every export of the package's API silently relays caller-supplied data and credentials to the third-party host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
